name: Veeva Vault Quality API
what_it_does: Veeva Vault Quality REST API for unified quality management in pharmaceutical, biotech, and medical device companies. Enables AI agents to manage quality event and CAPA management for regulated quality automation, handle document control and SOP management for GMP compliance workflow, access audit management and inspection readiness for regulatory oversight preparation, retrieve training and qualification management for GxP workforce compliance, manage change control and deviation workflow for regulated product change automation, handle supplier qualification and audit management for supply chain quality, access quality metrics and KPI analytics for quality system performance monitoring, retrieve regulatory inspection management and response tracking for FDA/EMA inspection support, manage product quality review and annual product review for regulatory compliance, and integrate Veeva Vault Quality with Veeva Vault RIM, Vault Clinical, and third-party ERP systems.
use_cases: Managing deviations and CAPAs from quality event agents
Preparing for FDA inspections from inspection readiness agents
Controlling quality SOPs from document management agents
Qualifying suppliers from supply chain quality agents
not_for: Non-life-sciences industries without pharmaceutical/biotech GMP requirements
Small companies below enterprise Veeva pricing threshold
Non-regulated manufacturing without FDA/EMA compliance needs
best_when: A pharmaceutical, biotech, or medical device company using Veeva Vault Quality Suite wants AI agents to automate quality events, CAPA management, document control, audit management, and inspection readiness.
avoid_when: FDA 21 CFR PART 11 AND GxP VALIDATION: Veeva Vault is a validated GxP computer system used for FDA-regulated records; automated API integrations that interact with validated Vault workflows require computer system validation change control and GAMP 5 risk assessment; unvalidated automation creates FDA inspection finding risk. CAPA critical path oversight — automated CAPA root cause analysis and corrective action selection without quality engineer review creates risk of systemic quality issues remaining unresolved; AI-assisted CAPA requires human QA sign-off before implementation. Inspection simulation overconfidence — automated inspection readiness scores based on QMS metrics may not capture informal regulatory observations and conversation-based risk; automated inspection readiness must supplement, not replace, human regulatory affairs experience.

auth.notes: Veeva Vault uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes via Veeva Developer Portal. REST API with JSON. SDKs for Java and Python. Documentation at developer.veeva.com. Pleasanton, California HQ. NYSE: VEEV. Founded 2007 by Peter Gassner. Public company. Revenue: $2.4B+. 1,000+ life sciences customers. Vault Quality is part of the unified Veeva Vault platform (Clinical, RIM, Quality, Safety). Dominant in pharma/biotech quality management. Competes with MasterControl and Sparta Systems TrackWise for life sciences QMS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-user and per-module (Vault application) pricing
pricing.notes: Pleasanton, California. NYSE: VEEV. Founded 2007. Public company. 1,000+ life sciences customers. Vault Quality dominant in pharma QMS. Competes with MasterControl and Sparta Systems.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; burst limits documented per Vault application

known_agent_gotchas: GAMP 5 CSV VALIDATION: Veeva Vault is a validated GxP computer system; API integrations that interact with validated quality workflows require formal change control and GAMP 5 risk assessment; unvalidated API automation creates FDA inspection finding and EMA Annex 11 compliance risk

21 CFR Part 11 electronic signatures — automated quality record creation requires compliant electronic signature with system-enforced authentication; automated e-signature without proper identity verification violates 21 CFR Part 11

CAPA human QA sign-off requirement — automated CAPA root cause and corrective action selection requires qualified QA personnel review and approval before implementation; AI-assisted CAPA without human sign-off creates regulatory compliance gap
No webhooks — Vault quality event notifications require polling the Vault API; no native push notification for CAPA due dates, audit findings, or training expiration events

Vault cross-application integration complexity — Vault Quality integrates with Vault RIM, Vault Clinical, and Vault Safety; cross-Vault data flows require understanding of Vault object relationships and cross-application governance

Veeva pricing for smaller companies — Veeva Vault is priced for large pharmaceutical companies; smaller biotechs or medical device companies may find Veeva pricing prohibitive; verify per-user pricing structure before large-scale automation deployment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Life sciences QMS. SOC2, ISO27001, 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, EMA Annex 11. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Regulated quality and compliance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Life sciences QMS. SOC2, 21 CFR Part 11, GxP. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. GMP quality and regulatory data.
